paradigm: Mk.XII Phase 3a gemma family-internal retry v2 — own 4 first canonical application + 2nd silent crash at 75% download
parent_axis: axis 109 (Qwen family-internal HQS-C MIXED) + axis 99 (Phase 3a 1/3 clean) + #245 next_cycle 2
supersedes: verdict.json (retry v1 OOM); BOTH retries failed with different signatures

fix_applied_in_v2.from_v1_OOM_diagnosis: default device_map loaded full fp16 in CPU RAM before sharding to GPU; container host RAM bounded ≤60GB
fix_applied_in_v2.v2_fix_1_loader: from_pretrained(torch_dtype=fp16, device_map='auto', low_cpu_mem_usage=True) — accelerate sharded load
fix_applied_in_v2.v2_fix_2_memory: explicit pod template request memInGb=251 (vs default ~60GB)
fix_applied_in_v2.v2_fix_4_sentinel: memory probe sentinels at every checkpoint (START/TOKENIZER/LOAD_START/LOAD_DONE/each-10-layers)

v2_crash_diagnosis.process_status: pid=217 disappeared (no python in ps aux post-crash)
ruled_out: OOM (memory.events oom=0, oom_kill=0; peak=42% of cgroup limit)
cgroup memory limit (251GB confirmed via /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max, not bounded)
remaining_hypotheses: (H1) xet (HuggingFace Rust download accelerator) Rust panic during chunked parallel download — log shows 51-61 concurrent connections, 130-150GB observed bytes sent across xet workers; xet panic may not surface to python wrapper
(H2) mfs (network filesystem mfs#us-ne-1.runpod.net:9421) transient I/O failure mid-write — /workspace is RTT-bound networked storage, sustained write throughput may have hiccup
(H3) HuggingFace Hub API rate limit triggered by 51-61 concurrent connections — could send SIGTERM-equivalent to python xet client
(H4) python silent SIGKILL by external runpod orchestrator (idle detection false positive, container resource arbiter) — no dmesg/syslog access from container to confirm
(H5) file descriptor exhaustion from xet's 51-61 worker pool

diagnostic_evidence.xet_log_excerpt: /workspace/.hf_cache/xet/logs/xet_20260427T130741412+0000_217.log shows continuous concurrency control updates with success_ratio=1.000 right up to 13:14:02 timestamp (last entry); python wrapper.log last update also 13:14
diagnostic_evidence.memcg_events: low=0 high=0 max=0 oom=0 oom_kill=0 oom_group_kill=0

own_4_compliance_self_audit.rule: training-resource troubleshooting MUST be root-cause + harden + evolve, NOT temporary workaround
own_4_compliance_self_audit.step_a_root_cause_diagnosis: DONE — see v2_crash_diagnosis above; ruled out OOM/disk/cgroup; 5 remaining hypotheses with diagnostic evidence
own_4_compliance_self_audit.step_b_canonical_helper_code_fix: PARTIAL — v2 fix (device_map='auto' + low_cpu_mem_usage=True) addressed v1 OOM root cause correctly, but introduced new failure mode (xet/mfs/HF-API). Step (b) for v3 retry: add HF_HUB_DISABLE_XET=1 + sequential download to canonical wrapper.
own_4_compliance_self_audit.step_c_defensive_harden: PARTIAL — v2 added memory probe sentinels (worked, captured 104GB peak), pre-flight cgroup query, RAM-aware pod spawn (251GB requested). v3 needs: try/except around from_pretrained with retry-on-network-error, snapshot_download stage separation, fd limit bump.

own_4_compliance_self_audit.mandatory_commit_gate: Verdict + own_4_self_audit + task #15 lands BEFORE retry v3 dispatch (this commit)

raw_10_caveats: Silent crash root cause UNDETERMINED — 5 hypotheses (xet panic / mfs hiccup / HF rate limit / runpod orchestrator SIGKILL / fd exhaustion) all consistent with evidence; no dmesg/syslog access to disambiguate.
v2 cost $0.397 is 'wasted' in terms of measurement output (0 cells gained), but produced canonical own 4 first application diagnostic record + 5 hypotheses + retry v3 plan.
v3 retry plan is DIAGNOSIS-DRIVEN (xet disable + sequential), not blind 'retry with bigger pod' or 'lower protocol' — own 4 anti-pattern checks pass.

Total dual-retry burn: $0.179 (v1) + $0.397 (v2) = $0.576 with 0 27b cells captured. Per own 4, cost burn IS expected during root-cause diagnosis cycles (legitimate vs anti-pattern of cap-bump retry without diagnosis).
raw#12 cherry-pick-immunity preserved: HMK-A/B/HQS-C verdicts stay INCONCLUSIVE_PERSISTENT, not promoted/retracted based on partial evidence.
